customer_anxiety_profile: Jared is not anxious in the traditional sense — he is avoidant. He has been delaying for months, is self-aware about it, and has a prior-evaluation disappointment that has not been resolved. He is cooperative once engaged but his default posture is to defer. The closer should expect warmth but should not mistake warmth for commitment.
unresolved_concerns: Prior evaluation: Jared said 'I wasn't all that impressed' at 00:41 — what specifically was unimpressive is unknown. This is the live objection the closer must surface and address before presenting the new design.
Bill not yet collected on the call — Jared volunteered to send it ahead of the appointment but has not done so yet. Closer should confirm receipt before the sit.
Confirmed appointment time is ambiguous — Brandon said '3PM' then '2PM' in the same breath at 02:50. Closer should confirm the time before the sit.
sensitive_topics_raised: Work schedule constraint — Jared is 'back on work duty' for the next week; the appointment window was chosen around this. Do not reschedule casually.
History of delays — Jared has apologized multiple times for not following through. The closer should not reference this history in a way that creates guilt.
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment booked for next Wednesday (nominally the 15th) at 3PM (time ambiguous — confirm before sit) via phone call.
Brandon sent Jared a text reminder during the call — Jared confirmed receipt at 02:25.
Appointment framed as 30-45 minutes to 'show you how our new program works and get you an updated evaluation.'
closer_should_know: Jared has seen a prior Ion evaluation and was not impressed — this is why he has been delaying for months. The closer must surface what was wrong with that evaluation in the first two minutes of the call and explicitly differentiate this presentation from it. Walking in with a generic pitch risks confirming Jared's prior impression and losing the sit mid-call.
